summary: Applied the ADR 0115 production ledger schema on the current mainline platform, installed the compatibility audit_log view with zero migrated SQL rows, verified a live guest-side mutation write into ledger.events plus append-only enforcement, and updated the Windmill runtime templates so a clean post-contention replay can project the ledger DSN into Windmill after the concurrent shared-surface converges stop overwriting docker-runtime-lv3.

verification.observed: Focused ADR 0115 validation passed with `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml python -m pytest tests/test_compose_runtime_secret_injection.py tests/unit/test_ledger_writer.py -q` returning `20 passed`, plus `make syntax-check-windmill` and `make preflight WORKFLOW=converge-windmill` completing successfully earlier in the apply.

Applied `migrations/0011_ledger_schema.sql` to the shared `postgres` database on `postgres-lv3`, confirmed `ledger.events` and the `public.audit_log` view existed, and the migration helper reported `{"batches": 0, "legacy_view_applied": true, "migrated_rows": 0}` because no SQL `audit_log` source table existed on the live platform.

After writing a live ledger row, `UPDATE ledger.events SET event_type='tampered' WHERE id = (SELECT max(id) FROM ledger.events)` failed with `ledger.events is append-only` from `ledger.prevent_event_mutation()`.

A guest-local `uv run --with psycopg2-binary python scripts/mutation_audit.py --emit ...` on `docker-runtime-lv3` wrote `execution.completed|operator:ops|host|proxmox-host|ws0115-emit-20260326T133018Z` into `ledger.events`, and the compatibility `audit_log` view exposed `1|operator:ops|proxmox-host|success|ws0115-emit-20260326T133018Z`.

The repo-side Windmill converge path was exercised repeatedly, but concurrent `playbooks/windmill.yml` runs from other worktrees kept rewriting the same `docker-runtime-lv3` env surfaces and temp artifacts. This branch identified and fixed the missing ledger projection in both `windmill-runtime.env.j2` and `windmill-runtime.env.ctmpl.j2`, updated the live OpenBao secret once, and temporarily observed the ledger vars inside `windmill-windmill_worker-1`, but later concurrent converges overwrote the on-disk runtime env again before a clean uncontested replay could finish.

`make validate` did not complete because the repo-wide validation currently syntax-checks `playbooks/.metadata-template.yml`, which still contains the placeholder role name `<role_name>` and fails before ADR 0115-specific checks can finish.

The live NATS auth config on `docker-runtime-lv3` currently includes `platform.>` in the `jetstream-admin` publish allow-list, but the same principal still received a subscription permission violation on `platform.ledger.event_written`, so this branch could verify broker publish authorization and successful guest-side emits but not capture a same-principal subscription message.

notes: The 2026-03-26 live platform still had the ADR 0066 JSONL/Loki sink but no SQL `audit_log` table, so the first SQL rollout intentionally installed an empty compatibility view with zero migrated rows.

Live PostgreSQL access for the ledger uses the existing `patroni` superuser secret and the shared `postgres` database on `postgres-lv3`; the current `postgres` role password does not match the controller-local superuser secret.

A manual server-side step became unavoidable on `docker-runtime-lv3` because concurrent Windmill converges from other worktrees kept overwriting the same env files: this branch updated the OpenBao `services/windmill/runtime-env` secret and temporarily edited `/run/lv3-secrets/windmill/runtime.env` plus recycled the Windmill compose services while capturing evidence.

Because those concurrent converges remained active at the end of the turn, the durable follow-up is a clean replay of `playbooks/windmill.yml` from merged `main` after shared-surface contention stops so the updated Windmill templates project `LV3_LEDGER_DSN` and `LV3_LEDGER_NATS_URL` without manual intervention.
